primary.question: What is the pass mark for HS831 linguistics and phonetics exam on the MSc Speech and Language Therapy (Pre-registration) programme?
primary.expected_answer: The student must achieve a pass mark of at least 74.
primary.actual_answer:
The pass mark for HS831 linguistics and phonetics exam is at least 74, which will be adjusted proportionately to represent in real terms the mark students would achieve if the module used the standard pass mark of 50 that applies to other modules.

Sources:
- [speechtherapy_25.pdf](https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/department-specific/speechtherapy_25.pdf)
- [speechtherapy_24-v1.pdf](https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2024-25/department-specific/speechtherapy_24-v1.pdf)

retrieval.retrieval_query: What is the pass mark for HS831 linguistics and phonetics exam on the MSc Speech and Language Therapy (Pre-registration) programme?

judge.justification: The answer provides the correct pass mark but includes unnecessary and inaccurate information about proportionate adjustments.

follow_up.question: What is the formula used to adjust the grade for HS831 linguistics and phonetics exam on this programme?
follow_up.expected_answer: Transformed grade = 1.92 x [raw grade – 74] + 50.
follow_up.actual_answer:
The formula used to adjust the grade for HS831 linguistics and phonetics exam is:

\[ \text{Transformed grade} = 1.92 \times [\text{raw grade} - 74] + 50 \]

This formula is applied to convert the specific pass mark of 74 into a proportional score if the standard pass mark of 50 was used instead.

Sources:
- [speechtherapy_25.pdf](https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/department-specific/speechtherapy_25.pdf)
- [speechtherapy_24-v1.pdf](https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2024-25/department-specific/speechtherapy_24-v1.pdf)

retrieval.retrieval_query: What is the formula used to adjust the grade for the HS831 linguistics and phonetics exam on the MSc Speech and Language Therapy (Pre-registration) programme?

judge.justification: The assistant's answer accurately and completely matches the reference formula provided.

follow_up.question: What are the progression requirements for students in the MSc Advanced Clinical Practitioner Apprenticeship pathway at the University of Essex?
follow_up.expected_answer: Progression is based on passing all assessments and all modules, with students required to pass all individual components of assessment.
follow_up.actual_answer:
Students registered on the MSc Advanced Clinical Practitioner Apprenticeship at the University of Essex are not eligible for a Postgraduate Diploma. There are no exit awards for this programme, but a request to transfer to the PG Dip Health Care Practice can be made.

follow_up.question: What is the process for reassessment of failed taught modules in these programmes?
follow_up.expected_answer: Students who have failed any taught module may have a second attempt at the assessment for the module(s); however, only one opportunity to resubmit the failed component will be offered.
follow_up.actual_answer:
Students who have failed any taught module may have a second attempt at the assessment for the module(s). Where all individual components of assessment must be passed in order to pass the module, only one opportunity to resubmit the failed component will be offered. This failed work will normally be resubmitted within four weeks of students receiving their marked work for the original attempt, except for clinical recordings which are typically resubmitted within six weeks and portfolios within around eight weeks.

Sources:
- [source_url: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/department-specific/pgcertpwp_25.pdf]

retrieval.retrieval_query: What is the process for reassessment of failed taught modules in the PG Cert Psychological Wellbeing Practice and PG Cert Specialist Adult Mental Health (Mental Health Wellbeing Practitioner) programmes?

judge.justification: The answer is mostly correct but includes additional details not in the reference (e.g., timeframes for resubmissions).

follow_up.question: What will be the module aggregate if a student's pre-resubmission module aggregate was above the pass mark and they resubmit their work?
follow_up.expected_answer: The module aggregate is capped at the pre-resubmission aggregate.
follow_up.actual_answer:
If the pre-resubmission module aggregate was above the pass mark of 40, then the resubmission cannot improve the module aggregate mark. The module aggregate is capped at the pre-resubmission aggregate.

follow_up.question: What happens if a student fails an assignment on second submission during in-year Board of Examiners meetings?
follow_up.expected_answer: An in-year Board of Examiners meeting may be convened, and the Board will either withdraw the student or offer additional assessment opportunities such as reassessment for uncapped module marks or voluntary reassessment.
follow_up.actual_answer:
If a student fails an assignment on second submission during an in-year Board of Examiners meeting, the Board will either withdraw the student or, if they submit valid Extenuating Circumstances, offer additional assessment opportunities such as reassessment for uncapped module marks or voluntary reassessment, in line with the Extenuating Circumstances Policy and guidelines.
